title: Shinji creates a new world in the ending of Evangelion 3010
display_title: Shinji creates a new world in the ending of Evangelion 3010
wikitext:
[]: Shinji creates a new world in the ending of Evangelion 3.0+1.0?             Quote Post by FrancoDaniel » Fri Mar 08, 2024 9:57 pm Does the rebuild one still exist or did it become the world where Shinji is at the station?

[]: Re: Shinji creates a new world in the ending of Evangelion 3.0+1.0?             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Sat Mar 09, 2024 12:53 pm I don't think so since you see the world going on, Asuka returns to Kensuke's house, etc To me the train station scene is mostly symbolical of the end of Shinji's, Eva's and even Anno's story.

[]: Re: Shinji creates a new world in the ending of Evangelion 3.0+1.0?             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Sat Mar 09, 2024 1:36 pm Just food for thought: a lot of disappointment with the way Thrice resolves its character relationships has to do with the characters, essentially, parting and not explicitly shown to be in line for a reunion. Perhaps it was decided that instead of prior endings where Shinji reconciles and integrates, the more fitting end to his character conflicts is to get as far away as is feasibly possible, and for Shinji, a fictional character, this involves the filmmakers deciding to use their unique powers to grant him a reality bounce. At the core of Shinji's character is the wish to not be where he is and that's basically where he ends up. Anno has previously willfully shut down the whole apparent "escapism is bad" message people have attributed to him and has said, actually, it can be a great experience overall to run away--Shinji is always shown to grow in these instances, they facilitate instead of prevent growth. Or perhaps in the case of Thrice, Shinji has earned the right to run away without it being an unhealthy decision, and this equates to freeing himself from the narrative without that being destructive to those around him and himself. In other words, I've seen some take issue with the fact that a narrative pre-occupied with the conflict of not running away then concludes that way ... but I find it hard to believe such a pointed decision was made in error. Or at least, I don't believe it's a contradiction.

[]: Re: Shinji creates a new world in the ending of Evangelion 3.0+1.0?             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sun Mar 10, 2024 5:53 pm FrancoDaniel wrote: Does the rebuild one still exist or did it become the world where Shinji is at the station?

[]: Re: Shinji creates a new world in the ending of Evangelion 3.0+1.0?             Quote Post by AsukaShikinami10 » Mon Mar 11, 2024 11:17 pm Well, at least it is implied that it's a different place from the rebuild world. Remember Shinji was stuck in the Minus Space and Mari reached out to him only for her Eva Unit to dissappear and leave them stranded there with no way for them to return to their original world. So my guess is that they ended up in a new world they created inside the Minus Space, 'cause there was no way for them to return.

[]: Re: Shinji creates a new world in the ending of Evangelion 3.0+1.0?             Quote Post by dzzthink » Tue Mar 12, 2024 7:21 pm Axx°N N. wrote: In other words, I've seen some take issue with the fact that a narrative pre-occupied with the conflict of not running away then concludes that way ... but I find it hard to believe such a pointed decision was made in error. Or at least, I don't believe it's a contradiction.

[]: Re: Shinji creates a new world in the ending of Evangelion 3.0+1.0?             Quote Post by Konja7 » Tue Mar 12, 2024 8:41 pm AsukaShikinami10 wrote: Well, at least it is implied that it's a different place from the rebuild world. Remember Shinji was stuck in the Minus Space and Mari reached out to him only for her Eva Unit to dissappear and leave them stranded there with no way for them to return to their original world. So my guess is that they ended up in a new world they created inside the Minus Space, 'cause there was no way for them to return.

[]: Re: Shinji creates a new world in the ending of Evangelion 3.0+1.0?             Quote Post by AdamMalkovitch » Tue Jul 30, 2024 8:01 pm It's left ambiguous afaik. 3+1 isn't afraid of breaking the fourth wall [literally, in the case of the shot of the glass breaking during Additional Impact and the horrifying "hyper-realistic" face of Evangelion Imaginary], so I always interpreted it like this- During Additional Impact, when Shinji, OG long-haired Rei, Mari, and Asuka are inside the Golgotha Object, they're between their fictional world as seen in the NTE movies, and our real world. The shots of Kaji's seed pods being deployed and fulfilling their function, along with the shots of the WUNDER's makeshift escape pods and the penguins at the shore near Village-3 shows that the NTE world does still exist. When Shinji uses the Golgotha Object to rewrite reality and erase EVAs from existence, the Object [possibly ceasing to exist, since it's been used] forces them into our reality, which IS a reality without EVAs, but not the world these characters want to be in. When Mari, Shinji, Rei, Kaworu, and Asuka are at the train station, they're still in Minus Space, but they're about to leave. That's why Mari came to save Shinji- in order to escape our fictional worlds, we very frequently need another person to pull us out of them, especially when we're young, and to these characters, our world is the fictional one- though Shinji, having completed his character arc and fully emotionally matured, hardly needs Mari's help. As Shinji and Mari are running through the live action shot of the city, they're still animated because they're not actually in our real world, obviously. But they're going back to their world through ours. Kaworu and Rei left aboard the train because they don't belong in either world, being alien to NTE's Earth and being fictional beings that can't exist in our world. Asuka, Mari, and Shinji still belong to their world though, so they're using ours as a corridor to return to theirs and begin Rebuilding in a world free of Evas, just like ours is.

[]: Re: Shinji creates a new world in the ending of Evangelion 3.0+1.0?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Sep 25, 2024 8:17 pm I'm pretty certain that Mari & Shinji are still in minus space at the end, and there's no timeskip either (why would he wear the DSS choker for 14 years?) When the art style goes to 'realistic' at the end that's when they reach the real world. The only reason ppl are seeing it as a reset ending is that it's such a popular fanfiction trope. It would serve no point but twist for the sake of twist and would undo the entire theme about how life endures in the ruined world & is still fighting for. We see Wille getting out of their escape pods, the world restored, Asuka arriving at the village etc. no point showing that if theyre getting erased from existence, and mari & Shinji fucking off to a separate world makes even less sense

[]: Re: Shinji creates a new world in the ending of Evangelion 3.0+1.0?             Quote Post by Konja7 » Thu Sep 26, 2024 9:35 am Kendrix wrote: We see Wille getting out of their escape pods, the world restored, Asuka arriving at the village etc. no point showing that if theyre getting erased from existence, and mari & Shinji fucking off to a separate world makes even less sense
